---
title: Assignment 04
author:
  - name: Kelly Sheng
    affiliations:
      - id: bu
        name: Boston University
        city: Boston
        state: MA
number-sections: true
date: '2025-10-08'
date-modified: today
date-format: long
format:
  html:
    theme: cerulean
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2

execute:
  echo: true
  eval: true
  freeze: auto
---

# Setup
```{python}
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.io as pio
import numpy as np

np.random.seed(42)

pio.renderers.default = "notebook+notebook_connected+vscode"

# Initialize Spark Session
spark = SparkSession.builder.appName("LightcastData").getOrCreate()

# Load Data
df = spark.read.option("header", "true").option("inferSchema", "true").option("multiLine","true").option("escape", "\"").csv("data/lightcast_job_postings.csv")

# # Show Schema and Sample Data
# print("---This is Diagnostic check, No need to print it in the final doc---")

# df.printSchema() # comment this line when rendering the submission
# df.show(5)
```

## Cleaning and variable selection
```{python}
# | eval: true
from pyspark.sql.functions import when, col, pow, lower, trim
from pyspark.ml.feature import StringIndexer, OneHotEncoder, VectorAssembler
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline
from pyspark.ml.regression import LinearRegression
from pyspark.ml.evaluation import RegressionEvaluator

cont_vars = ["MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE", "MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE", "DURATION", "MIN_EDULEVELS"]
cat_vars = ["EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME", "REMOTE_TYPE_NAME"]
y = ["SALARY"]

median_duration = df.approxQuantile("DURATION", [0.5], 0.01)[0]

df = df.withColumn(
    "DURATION",
    when(col("DURATION").isNull(), median_duration)
    .otherwise(col("DURATION"))
)

median_salary = df.approxQuantile("SALARY", [0.5], 0.01)[0]

df = df.withColumn(
    "SALARY",
    when(
        col("SALARY").isNull() &
        (col("SALARY_TO").isNull() | col("SALARY_FROM").isNull()),
        median_salary
    )
    .when(col("SALARY").isNull() & col("SALARY_TO").isNull(), col("SALARY_FROM"))
    .when(col("SALARY").isNull() & col("SALARY_FROM").isNull(), col("SALARY_TO"))
    .when(
        col("SALARY").isNull() &
        col("SALARY_TO").isNotNull() &
        col("SALARY_FROM").isNotNull(),
        (col("SALARY_TO") + col("SALARY_FROM")) / 2
    )
    .otherwise(col("SALARY"))
)

df = df.withColumn(
    "REMOTE_TYPE_NAME",
    when(
        col("REMOTE_TYPE_NAME").isNull() | (trim(col("REMOTE_TYPE_NAME")) == "") | (trim(lower(col("REMOTE_TYPE_NAME"))).rlike(r"\[?none\]?")),
        "Undefined"
    )
    .when(lower(col("REMOTE_TYPE_NAME")).contains("not remote"), "Onsite")
    .when(lower(col("REMOTE_TYPE_NAME")).contains("hybrid"), "Hybrid")
    .otherwise(col("REMOTE_TYPE_NAME"))
)

df = df.withColumn(
    "EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME",
    when(col("EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME") == "Part-time / full-time", "Flexible")
    .when(col("EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME") == "Part-time (â‰¤ 32 hours)", "Part-time")
    .when(col("EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME") == "Full-time (> 32 hours)", "Full-time")
    .when(col("EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME").isNull(), "Fulltime")
    .otherwise(col("EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME")
))

df = df.withColumn("MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE_SQ", pow(col("MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE"), 2))

df = df.withColumn(
    "MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE",
    when(
        col("MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE").isNull() & col("MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE").isNotNull(),
        col("MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE") + 1 
    ).otherwise(col("MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE"))
)

df = df.dropna(subset = cont_vars + cat_vars + y).select(cont_vars + cat_vars + y)
```
Four continuous variables were selected based on their relevance to compensation. Null entries were imputed using median values and logical rules in order to maximize the number of valid entries to include in the training set. Categorical variables were encoded using StringIndexer and OneHotEncoder, converting them into numeric vectors suitable for model input.

# Feature Engineering
```{python}
# | eval: true
indexers = [StringIndexer(inputCol=c, outputCol=f"{c}_idx", handleInvalid="skip") for c in cat_vars]
encoders = [OneHotEncoder(inputCol=f"{c}_idx", outputCol=f"{c}_vec") for c in cat_vars]

assembler = VectorAssembler(
    inputCols=cont_vars + [f"{c}_vec" for c in cat_vars],
    outputCol="features"
)

pipeline = Pipeline(stages=indexers + encoders + [assembler])

df = pipeline.fit(df).transform(df)
```

# Train/Test Split
```{python}
# | eval: true
train_X, test_X = df.randomSplit([0.8, 0.2], seed = 17)
print((df.count(), len(df.columns)))
print((train_X.count(), len(train_X.columns)))
print((test_X.count(), len(test_X.columns)))
```

# Linear Regression
```{python}
# | eval: true
from pyspark.ml.regression import GeneralizedLinearRegression

reg_r2 = RegressionEvaluator(labelCol="SALARY", predictionCol="prediction", metricName="r2")
reg_rmse = RegressionEvaluator(labelCol="SALARY", predictionCol="prediction", metricName="rmse")
reg_mae = RegressionEvaluator(labelCol="SALARY", predictionCol="prediction", metricName="mae")

glr = GeneralizedLinearRegression(featuresCol="features", labelCol="SALARY", family="gaussian", link="identity", maxIter=10, regParam=0.3)
glr_model = glr.fit(train_X)
glr_summary = glr_model.summary
glr_pred = glr_model.transform(test_X)

glr_r2 = reg_r2.evaluate(glr_pred)
glr_rmse = reg_rmse.evaluate(glr_pred)
glr_mae = reg_mae.evaluate(glr_pred)

print(f"\nIntercept: {glr_model.intercept:.4f}")
print(f"R²: {glr_r2:.4f}")
print(f"RMSE: {glr_rmse:.4f}")
print(f"MAE: {glr_mae:.4f}")
    
data = list(zip(
    assembler.getInputCols(),
    glr_model.coefficients,
    glr_summary.coefficientStandardErrors,
    glr_summary.tValues,
    glr_summary.pValues
))

coef_df = pd.DataFrame(data, columns=["Feature", "Coefficient", "Std_Error", "t_Value", "p_Value"])

coef_df["Coefficient"] = coef_df["Coefficient"].round(4)
coef_df["Std_Error"] = coef_df["Std_Error"].round(4)
coef_df["t_Value"] = coef_df["t_Value"].round(4)
coef_df["p_Value"] = coef_df["p_Value"].round(4)

coef_df
```

# Polynomial Regression
```{python}
poly_df = df.withColumn("MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE_SQ", pow(col("MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE"), 2))

assembler_poly = VectorAssembler(
    inputCols=["MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE_SQ"] + cont_vars + [f"{c}_vec" for c in cat_vars],
    outputCol="features_poly"
)

poly_df = assembler_poly.transform(poly_df)

train_poly, test_poly = poly_df.randomSplit([0.8, 0.2], seed=17)

poly_glr = GeneralizedLinearRegression(
    featuresCol="features_poly",
    labelCol="SALARY",
    family="gaussian",
    link="identity",
    maxIter=10,
    regParam=0.3
)
poly_model = poly_glr.fit(train_poly)
poly_summary = poly_model.summary
poly_pred = poly_model.transform(test_poly)

poly_r2 = reg_r2.evaluate(poly_pred)
poly_rmse = reg_rmse.evaluate(poly_pred)
poly_mae = reg_mae.evaluate(poly_pred)

print(f"\nPolynomial Regression Intercept: {poly_model.intercept:.4f}")
print(f"Polynomial R²: {poly_r2:.4f}")
print(f"Polynomial RMSE: {poly_rmse:.4f}")
print(f"Polynomial MAE: {poly_mae:.4f}")

poly_data = list(zip(
    assembler_poly.getInputCols(),
    poly_model.coefficients,
    poly_summary.coefficientStandardErrors,
    poly_summary.tValues,
    poly_summary.pValues
))

poly_coef_df = pd.DataFrame(poly_data, columns=["Feature", "Coefficient", "Std_Error", "t_Value", "p_Value"])
poly_coef_df = poly_coef_df.round(4)
poly_coef_df
```

# Random Forest Regressor
```{python}
from pyspark.ml.regression import RandomForestRegressor

rf = RandomForestRegressor(
    featuresCol="features",
    labelCol="SALARY",
    numTrees=200,
    maxDepth=7,
    seed=17
)

rf_model = rf.fit(train_X)
rf_pred = rf_model.transform(test_X)

rf_r2 = reg_r2.evaluate(rf_pred)
rf_rmse = reg_rmse.evaluate(rf_pred)
rf_mae = reg_mae.evaluate(rf_pred)

print(f"\nRandom Forest R²: {rf_r2:.4f}")
print(f"Random Forest RMSE: {rf_rmse:.4f}")
print(f"Random Forest MAE: {rf_mae:.4f}")
```

## Feature Importance Plot
``` {python}
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import os

importances = rf_model.featureImportances.toArray()
feature_names = assembler.getInputCols()

expanded_feature_names = []
for name in feature_names:
    if name.endswith("_vec"):
        expanded_feature_names.append(f"{name}_encoded")
    else:
        expanded_feature_names.append(name)

# If lengths don't match, truncate or pad safely
min_len = min(len(importances), len(expanded_feature_names))
expanded_feature_names = expanded_feature_names[:min_len]
importances = importances[:min_len]

# Create DataFrame
fi_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": expanded_feature_names,
    "Importance": importances
}).sort_values("Importance", ascending=False)

# Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
sns.barplot(data=fi_df, x="Importance", y="Feature", palette="crest")
plt.title("Feature Importances (Random Forest)")
plt.savefig(os.path.join("output", "rf_feature_importance.png"))
plt.show()
```

# Comparison between models
```{python}
glr_pred = glr_model.transform(test_X)
glr_rmse_val = reg_rmse.evaluate(glr_pred)
glr_aic_val = glr_model.summary.aic
glr_bic_val = glr_model.summary.deviance + np.log(test_X.count()) * len(glr_model.coefficients)

glr_results = glr_pred.select(col("SALARY").alias("actual"), col("prediction").alias("pred_glr"))

poly_pred = poly_model.transform(test_poly)
poly_rmse_val = reg_rmse.evaluate(poly_pred)
poly_aic_val = poly_model.summary.aic
poly_bic_val = poly_model.summary.deviance + np.log(test_poly.count()) * len(poly_model.coefficients)

poly_results = poly_pred.select(col("SALARY").alias("actual"), col("prediction").alias("pred_poly"))

rf_pred = rf_model.transform(test_X)
rf_rmse_val = reg_rmse.evaluate(rf_pred)
rf_aic_val = None # AIC and BIC are not applicable for random forest models
rf_bic_val = None # AIC and BIC are not applicable for random forest models

rf_results = rf_pred.select(col("SALARY").alias("actual"), col("prediction").alias("pred_rf"))

print("GLR: RMSE =", glr_rmse_val, "AIC =", glr_aic_val, "BIC =", glr_bic_val)
print("Poly GLR: RMSE =", poly_rmse_val, "AIC =", poly_aic_val, "BIC =", poly_bic_val)
print("Random Forest: RMSE =", rf_rmse_val, "AIC =", rf_aic_val, "BIC =", rf_bic_val)
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

glr_df = glr_results.toPandas()
poly_df = poly_results.toPandas()
rf_df = rf_results.toPandas()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 10))
fig.suptitle("Actual vs. Predicted Salary: Model Comparison", fontsize=16)

# GLR
sns.scatterplot(ax=axes[0,0], x=glr_df["actual"], y=glr_df["pred_glr"], alpha=0.5)
axes[0,0].plot([glr_df["actual"].min(), glr_df["actual"].max()],
               [glr_df["actual"].min(), glr_df["actual"].max()],
               color="red", linestyle="--")
axes[0,0].set_title("GLR")
axes[0,0].set_xlabel("Actual Salary")
axes[0,0].set_ylabel("Predicted Salary")

# Poly GLR
sns.scatterplot(ax=axes[0,1], x=poly_df["actual"], y=poly_df["pred_poly"], alpha=0.5)
axes[0,1].plot([poly_df["actual"].min(), poly_df["actual"].max()],
               [poly_df["actual"].min(), poly_df["actual"].max()],
               color="red", linestyle="--")
axes[0,1].set_title("Polynomial GLR")
axes[0,1].set_xlabel("Actual Salary")
axes[0,1].set_ylabel("Predicted Salary")

# Random Forest
sns.scatterplot(ax=axes[1,0], x=rf_df["actual"], y=rf_df["pred_rf"], alpha=0.5)
axes[1,0].plot([rf_df["actual"].min(), rf_df["actual"].max()],
               [rf_df["actual"].min(), rf_df["actual"].max()],
               color="red", linestyle="--")
axes[1,0].set_title("Random Forest")
axes[1,0].set_xlabel("Actual Salary")
axes[1,0].set_ylabel("Predicted Salary")

axes[1,1].axis("off")

plt.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0.03, 1, 0.95])
plt.savefig(os.path.join("output", "actual_vs_predicted.png"))
plt.show()
```
Interpretation

Comparison of these values reveals that, between the three models, the Random Forest model achieved the best performance, reducing RMSE by nearly 5% compared to polynomial regression.

Random Forest also had the lowest AIC and BIC values which indicates a good trade-off between model complexity and fit.

Linear and polynomial models revealed interpretable relationships, but the data's interactions were more complex than these models allowed, resulting in only ~12% of explainable variance in salary. Random Forest was able to capture these patterns more effectively.

## Calculating log-likelihood and BIC
```{python}
from math import log

def calc_log_likelihood(y_true, y_pred, sigma2):
    n = len(y_true)
    ll = -0.5 * n * log(2 * np.pi * sigma2) - 0.5 * np.sum((y_true - y_pred) ** 2) / sigma2
    return ll

def get_y_true_pred(results_df, pred_col):
    pdf = results_df.select("actual", pred_col).toPandas()
    y_true = pdf["actual"].values
    y_pred = pdf[pred_col].values
    return y_true, y_pred

y_true_glr, y_pred_glr = get_y_true_pred(glr_results, "pred_glr")
sigma2_glr = np.var(y_true_glr - y_pred_glr)
ll_glr = calc_log_likelihood(y_true_glr, y_pred_glr, sigma2_glr)
aic_glr = 2 * len(glr_model.coefficients) - 2 * ll_glr
bic_glr = log(len(y_true_glr)) * len(glr_model.coefficients) - 2 * ll_glr

y_true_poly, y_pred_poly = get_y_true_pred(poly_results, "pred_poly")
sigma2_poly = np.var(y_true_poly - y_pred_poly)
ll_poly = calc_log_likelihood(y_true_poly, y_pred_poly, sigma2_poly)
aic_poly = 2 * len(poly_model.coefficients) - 2 * ll_poly
bic_poly = log(len(y_true_poly)) * len(poly_model.coefficients) - 2 * ll_poly

y_true_rf, y_pred_rf = get_y_true_pred(rf_results, "pred_rf")
sigma2_rf = np.var(y_true_rf - y_pred_rf)
ll_rf = calc_log_likelihood(y_true_rf, y_pred_rf, sigma2_rf)
n_rf_params = len(rf_model.featureImportances)
aic_rf = 2 * n_rf_params - 2 * ll_rf
bic_rf = log(len(y_true_rf)) * n_rf_params - 2 * ll_rf

print(f"GLR: log-likelihood={ll_glr:.2f}, AIC={aic_glr:.2f}, BIC={bic_glr:.2f}")
print(f"Poly GLR: log-likelihood={ll_poly:.2f}, AIC={aic_poly:.2f}, BIC={bic_poly:.2f}")
print(f"Random Forest: log-likelihood={ll_rf:.2f}, AIC={aic_rf:.2f}, BIC={bic_rf:.2f}")
```
Higher log-likelihood values for the Random Forest also indicate a better fit to observed data.
